questions.text: Which of the following elements does not show allotropy in Group 15?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen is the only element in Group 15 that does not show allotropy under normal conditions.

questions.text: The maximum covalency of Nitrogen is:
questions.explanation: Nitrogen lacks d-orbitals in its valence shell, so its covalency is restricted to 4 (as in NH₄⁺).

questions.text: Which of the following Group 15 hydrides is the strongest reducing agent?
questions.explanation: Reducing character increases down the group as M-H bond strength decreases; BiH₃ is least stable.

questions.text: Nitrogen is chemically inert at room temperature due to:
questions.explanation: The triple bond (N≡N) is extremely strong and requires high energy to break.

questions.text: In the Haber process for ammonia, the catalyst used is:
questions.explanation: Finely divided iron with small amounts of K₂O and Al₂O₃ (previously Mo) is used as a catalyst.

questions.text: The brown ring test is used for the detection of:
questions.explanation: It depends on the reduction of nitrate to nitric oxide, which reacts with Fe²⁺ to form a brown complex.

questions.text: Which allotrope of phosphorus is most reactive?
questions.explanation: White phosphorus is highly reactive due to angular strain in P₄ molecules (bond angle 60°).

questions.text: PH₃ (Phosphine) has a ________ smell.
questions.explanation: Pure phosphine is non-inflammable but it has a characteristic rotten fish odor.

questions.text: Which of the following is called 'laughing gas'?
questions.explanation: Nitrous oxide (N₂O) is known as laughing gas.

questions.text: Ozone is an allotrope of:
questions.explanation: Ozone (O₃) is a triatomic allotrope of oxygen.

questions.text: In Group 16, the boiling point increases down the group from H₂S to H₂Te, but H₂O has an abnormally high boiling point due to:
questions.explanation: Strong intermolecular hydrogen bonding in water makes it a liquid with a high boiling point.

questions.text: The catalyst used in the Contact Process for sulfuric acid is:
questions.explanation: Vanadium pentoxide (V₂O₅) is used for the oxidation of SO₂ to SO₃.

questions.text: Which of the following is a 'super-halogen'?
questions.explanation: Fluorine is the most electronegative and reactive of all halogens.

questions.text: Electron gain enthalpy of Chlorine is higher than that of Fluorine because:
options: Small size of F leads to inter-electronic repulsions
questions.explanation: The small size of the 2p orbital in F makes the incoming electron feel significant repulsion from the electrons already present.

questions.text: Bleaching action of Chlorine is due to:
questions.explanation: Chlorine in the presence of moisture releases nascent oxygen, which oxidizes colored matter to colorless.

questions.text: Interhalogen compounds are more reactive than halogens (except F₂) because:
options: X-X' bond is weaker than X-X bond
questions.explanation: The polarity of the X-X' bond makes it easier to break than the non-polar X-X bond.

questions.text: Which noble gas has the lowest boiling point of any known substance?
questions.explanation: Helium (4.2 K) has the lowest boiling point due to extremely weak dispersion forces.

questions.text: The first noble gas compound synthesized by Neil Bartlett was:
questions.explanation: He reacted Xenon with PtF₆ after observing PtF₆ could oxidize molecular oxygen.

questions.text: The shape of XeF₂ is:
questions.explanation: According to VSEPR theory, it has 2 bond pairs and 3 lone pairs in equatorial positions, making it linear.

questions.text: The oxidation state of Oxygen in OF₂ is:
questions.explanation: Since Fluorine is more electronegative than Oxygen, O shows a +2 oxidation state here.

questions.text: Which Group 15 element forms the most basic hydride?
questions.explanation: NH₃ is the most basic because the lone pair is concentrated on a small nitrogen atom.

questions.text: Sulphuric acid is known as:
questions.explanation: It was historically obtained by distilling green vitriol and is called Oil of Vitriol.

questions.text: Which noble gas is used in diving equipment to dilute oxygen?
questions.explanation: Helium is less soluble in blood than Nitrogen, preventing 'bends' when divers resurface.

questions.text: Catenation tendency is maximum in:
questions.explanation: The S-S bond is stronger than the O-O bond due to lone pair repulsions in O-O.

questions.text: Which of the following oxoacids of sulfur contains a peroxide link?
questions.explanation: H₂S₂O₈ (Marshall's acid) contains a peroxo group (-O-O-).

questions.text: In the reaction of copper with dilute HNO₃, the gas evolved is:
questions.explanation: 3Cu + 8dil.HNO₃ → 3Cu(NO₃)₂ + 2NO + 4H₂O.

questions.text: Noble gases are mostly chemically inert because:
questions.explanation: They have stable ns²np⁶ configurations and very high energies are required to remove electrons.

questions.text: Helium is used in balloons because it is:
questions.explanation: It is safer than Hydrogen because it does not burn.

questions.text: The hybridization of Xe in XeF₄ is:
questions.explanation: Xe has 4 bond pairs and 2 lone pairs, requiring 6 hybrid orbitals (sp³d²).

questions.text: Chlorine reacts with excess ammonia to give:
questions.explanation: 8NH₃ (excess) + 3Cl₂ → 6NH₄Cl + N₂.

questions.text: PCl₅ exists in the solid state as:
questions.explanation: In solid state, it ionicizes into tetrahedral [PCl₄]⁺ and octahedral [PCl₆]⁻.

questions.text: Which halogen is a liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Bromine is a reddish-brown liquid at standard conditions.

questions.text: Which element has the highest negative electron gain enthalpy in the periodic table?
questions.explanation: Chlorine releases the most energy when it accepts an electron.

questions.text: HF is a liquid while other hydrogen halides are gases because of:
questions.explanation: Strong H-bonding results in association of HF molecules.

questions.text: The strongest oxoacid among the following is:
questions.explanation: Acidic strength increases with increase in the oxidation state of the central atom.

questions.text: Which of the following is an amphoteric oxide?
questions.explanation: Oxides of As and Sb are amphoteric; N and P are acidic, Bi is basic.

questions.text: Aqua regia is a mixture of:
questions.explanation: It is a 3:1 molar mixture of conc. HCl and conc. HNO₃ used to dissolve noble metals.

questions.text: Which of the following is paramagnetic in gaseous state?
questions.explanation: In vapor state, sulfur exists as S₂ which has two unpaired electrons like O₂.

questions.text: Red phosphorus is less reactive than white phosphorus because:
questions.explanation: Red phosphorus consists of chains of P₄ tetrahedra linked together.

questions.text: The structure of white phosphorus is:
questions.explanation: It exists as discrete P₄ tetrahedral molecules.
